Recognition has been accorded
the two federal unions and the
teamsters by the North Coast rail-
way in the erection Of the termin-
ais in this city, in the event that
the franchise 10 passed and the
road admitted.


In the first proposition submitted
to organized labor by Mr.. strahorn
these unions were omitted, and
loud kick went up from this quar-
ter. Mr.. Strahorn was appe aled to
and made reply in the following
manner:


SPOKANE, Wash, April 1.
191O.-Messrs Chavez and Cliff,
Business Agents Federal Unions
No. 1922 and No. 1924, City.


Gentlemen: Pursuant to your
request for a statement of the posi-
ton of the North Coast Railroad
company in regard to the recogni-
ton of your unions in work to be
done in this city, beg to advise
that in the wrecking of all build
ings done by the North Coast Rail-
road company and in all eXcavating
for buildings constructed by the
company within the city Of Spo.
knae, the North Coast Railroad
company will at all times recognize
the jurisdiction of the building
trades unions affiliated with the
Spokane Building Trades council,
the Team Owners union, the
Transfers union, and the Federal
union No. 1924 and Federal union
No. 1929.


81 thought that my previous state
ments had made my position clear
upon the question of union labor,
but I am glad of this opportunity
to express myself in regard to the
unions in which you are specially
interested. Yours very truly, Rob-
ert E. Strahorn."
